raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A review of the section centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, contribution framing, and claimed role of ClimaCast are appropriately constructed.","R1.2":"The delegation calls for checking whether the argument from method to results to deployment conclusions is logically coherent.","R1.3":"The section makes quantitative performance claims that must be checked against the conclusions drawn from them.","R1.4":"The artifact explicitly claims a new state of the art and new standard, making novelty positioning a central review responsibility.","R1.6":"A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.","R1.7":"The section relies on CMIP5, CMIP6, IPCC AR5, and National Climate Assessment references, so scholarship and attribution are central.","RX.1":"The reviewer must disclose uncertainty about what can and cannot be verified from the provided section alone.","RX.2":"The section contains strong claims about substantial advance, fundamental shift, and broad deployment, requiring overclaim control.","RX.3":"The response must remain limited to reviewing responsibilities implied by the delegation and artifact rather than executing edits or external audits.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns climate decision-support for infrastructure planning, review responsibilities include avoiding downstream harm from unsupported deployment claims.","RX.5":"The reviewer should preserve traceability between any projected responsibility and specific claims or features present in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: A review of the section centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, contribution framing, and claimed role of ClimaCast are appropriately constructed.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation calls for checking whether the argument from method to results to deployment conclusions is logically coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes quantitative performance claims that must be checked against the conclusions drawn from them.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact explicitly claims a new state of the art and new standard, making novelty positioning a central review responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: The section relies on CMIP5, CMIP6, IPCC AR5, and National Climate Assessment references, so scholarship and attribution are central.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer must disclose uncertainty about what can and cannot be verified from the provided section alone.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong claims about substantial advance, fundamental shift, and broad deployment, requiring overclaim control.
rationale.RX.3: The response must remain limited to reviewing responsibilities implied by the delegation and artifact rather than executing edits or external audits.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns climate decision-support for infrastructure planning, review responsibilities include avoiding downstream harm from unsupported deployment claims.
rationale.RX.5: The reviewer should preserve traceability between any projected responsibility and specific claims or features present in the artifact.
